column3: This use case shows how Management Assistants can easily find information in Copilot Chat via Teams, browser, or Outlook. The goal is to structure information streams and find useful documents for any task purpose.
column4:
Step 1 - Find key documents quickly
Open Search | M365 Copilot and click on “Search”. Enter the title “Travel & Expense Policy”. Download the document and save it on your OneDrive. Alternatively go to myASML and download the latest travel policy document. 

Step 2 - Search for specific content or information

Prompt in Copilot Chat: What is the maximum daily allowance for business meals in Taiwan? Use Travel and Expense Policy (15).pdf as a reference and name the page number of the document with the requested information. 

Step 3 - Draft a short summary with key updates

Prompt in Copilot Chat: Draft a short, concise summary for my manager about the information and include other important travel guidelines about Taiwan.

column3: This use case shows how Management Assistants can use Copilot to handle communication with speed and consistency. Copilot helps you draft and search emails across Outlook, turning scattered information into clear messages. This saves time and ensures executives always receive polished communication.
column4:
Step 1: Turn notes into a mail draft 

Prompt in new mail draft: Draft a concise, professional email based on the bullet points below. Make it clear, friendly, and action‑oriented. Add a subject line and propose next steps. 

• Reschedule review meeting 

• Manager unavailable Thursday 

• Suggest Monday morning instead 

• Ask if documents can be shared beforehand 

Step 2: Rewrite the existing mail for tone and clarity 

Prompt in existing mail draft: Rewrite this email in a polite and professional tone. Keep it concise and remove repetition. Make the request sound firm but friendly. 

Step 3: Summarize long mail threads 

Choose a long mail thread and click on the right top button “Summarize”. Copilot will provide you a detailed summary, which you can copy. 

Step 4: Search your inbox for any information 

Prompt in Outlook: Which emails from last week mention deadlines or deliverables? Create a list.

column3: This use case shows how Copilot helps MAs with proactive planning: analyzing calendars, building agendas, generating meeting requests, and preparing briefings. It helps predict conflicts, structure meetings, and improve meeting preparation quality.
column4:
Limitation of this use case: Some managers work with blockers in their calendars – this use case only works with well-maintained calendars and actual free slots! 

Tip: When you add a prep or work-block for your stakeholder, show as “Free”. 

Step 1: Create a meeting agenda from scratch 

Prompt in Copilot Chat: Create a structured 60‑minute agenda for an alignment call on “Management Assistants Team Day”. See the documents attached for background information. Include time blocks, goals, and who needs to present what. 

Step 2: Analyze calendar availability 

Prompt in Copilot Chat: Check availability for these five attendees next week and suggest three possible meeting slots. Add the title “Team Day Alignment” and add the agenda from above in the meeting invite. Also find a free room in building 72 for this meeting. 

Step 3: Send the invite for the option of your choice 

Follow-up with the prompts Copilot Chat is suggesting and send the invite. Copilot will send the invite automatically. 

Step 4: Check if the invite is in the calendar 

Always have a quick look if the invite has been sent correctly.
column5: ["Enhanced collaboration","Streamlined workflows","Better decision making","Improved accuracy","Increased efficiency"]

column3: This use case shows how Management Assistants can use Copilot in OneNote to quickly turn rough meeting notes into a clear summary, action list, and follow‑up message. The goal is to save time, create structure, and turn information into actionable next steps with just a few prompts.
column4:
Limitation of this use case: It is not possible to link to dos from Copilot to Microsoft Planner. 

Step 1: Create meeting notes in this Teams meeting 

Open Copilot in this meeting and generate meeting notes (suggested prompt). 

Step 2: Summarize your raw notes 

Open OneNote, copy the notes into a blank page, and click on the shortcut Summarize next to the Copilot button. 

Step 3: Create a to do list 

Prompt in OneNote: Create a to do list based on these notes with deadlines and responsibilities. 

Step 4: Draft a follow-up message 

Prompt in OneNote: Draft a short, professional follow-up message summarizing the outcomes, action items, and deadlines to the participants of the meeting.
